text:
15/05/2024

Oncology Department
University Hospital

Dear Dr. Smith,

Re: Patient ID 884, 62-year-old male

I am writing to update you on the status of your patient. The patient was initially diagnosed with lung adenocarcinoma in the upper lobe back in January 2022. Pathological examination measured the tumor at 35 mm. He underwent a right upper lobectomy on the first of February 2022.

A brain metastasis was identified in February 2023. He received stereotactic radiation therapy for the brain lesion in March of last year.

Unfortunately, the disease has progressed as of last month. The CT scan performed five days ago confirmed the progression in the liver. A liver biopsy was performed last week to confirm the nature of the new lesions. Subsequent imaging confirmed liver metastasis at the end of last month. Biomarker analysis from the recent biopsy returned positive for PD-L1 expression.

Today, his weight is stable at 78 kg. Clinical evaluation indicates a performance status of 1. His medical history is notable for essential hypertension managed for several years. We are initiating treatment with pembrolizumab today.

We will monitor his response closely.

Sincerely,

Dr. Evans
Oncologist
